section_title: Power of companies to refer matters to arbitration.-(1) A company may by written agreement, refer to
content:
227. Power of companies to refer matters to arbitration.-(1) A company may by written agreement, refer to
arbitration, in accordance with the arbitration act, 1940 (X of 1940), on existing or future difference between itself and any
other company or person.
(2) Companies may delegate to the arbitrator power to settle any terms or to determine any matter capable of being lawfully
settled or determined by the companies themselves, or by their director or other managing body.
(3) The provision of the Arbitration Act, 1940 (X of 1940), shall apply to all arbitration's between companies and persons in
pursuance of this Act.

sub_sections.text:
A company may by written agreement, refer to
arbitration, in accordance with the arbitration act, 1940 (X of 1940), on existing or future difference between itself and any
other company or person.

Companies may delegate to the arbitrator power to settle any terms or to determine any matter capable of being lawfully
settled or determined by the companies themselves, or by their director or other managing body.

The provision of the Arbitration Act, 1940 (X of 1940), shall apply to all arbitration's between companies and persons in
pursuance of this Act.
